creator: bart@bartsplace.net
text:
System crashes, seemingly at random, tho it seems usually during or just after relatively heavy disk load.

In all cases the crash looks identical (happens at the same bit of code, with always the same process (11, [idle])

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

kernel config is a copy of GENERIC with the following options added:

options DUMMYNET
options IPFIREWALL_FORWARD
options IPDIVERT
options IPFIREWALL_VERBOSE_LIMIT=1000
options IPFIREWALL_VERBOSE
options IPFIREWALL

This is to enable IPFIREWALL_FORWARD, which is an absolutely mandatory feature for this configuration.

If any other information is needed, I have multiple crashdumps available.

Fix: 

Not a fix but a workaround, using 4BSD scheduler does not result in crashes so far.
How-To-Repeat: No known scenario, other then leaving the machine powered on, and compiling or running software.
